steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
Since both equations are already solved for $y$, we can set their right-hand sides equal:
$$-10x + 13 = -12x + 15$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Collect all $x$ terms on the left and constants on the right:
$$-10x + 12x = 15 - 13$$
$$2x = 2$$
$$x = 1$$

steps.title: Find y and identify the solution
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 1$ into the first equation:
$$y = -10(1) + 13 = -10 + 13 = 3$$
The solution $(1, 3)$ matches Choice D — $(1,3)$ is correct.
